title: Simplify resume logic to continue the exact active transaction
goal: Implement the plan-defined rule that resume continues the exact active transaction from healthy materialized state using top-level next_action as the primary continuation guide, with event-log rebuild used only when materialized state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent, and with explicit failure when exact deterministic continuation is not safely possible.
scope: Make healthy materialized state the primary resume anchor.
Define and enforce the exact conditions under which materialized state is considered healthy enough for direct resume.
Use event-history rebuild only under the fallback conditions described in plan.md: when materialized state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.
Ensure resume reuses the exact active tx_id and does not replace it with heuristic candidates.
Ensure valid top-level next_action is the primary continuation guide over status-derived heuristics and reconstructed guesses.
Ensure post-terminal work is not resumed as active work.
Ensure malformed or ambiguous canonical persistence causes explicit resume failure rather than silent fallback or heuristic active-transaction replacement.

Review and align exact-resume behavior across the runtime surfaces that participate in continuation guidance and helper behavior, including commit_manager.py, ops_tools.py, repo_tools.py, workflow_response.py, and fallback-only rebuild logic in state_rebuilder.py.
Align ops_* helper behavior with exact-active-transaction continuation and next_action-first resume rules without promoting helper labels or session context into canonical transaction identity.
out_of_scope: Changing tx_id issuance semantics outside p2-t01.
Broad compatibility redesign outside bounded behavior.
Introducing extra continuation metadata not present in plan.md.
Historical in-place rewrite of prior logs or snapshots.
Redefining session context as canonical transaction identity.
expected_outputs: Runtime changes that continue the exact active transaction deterministically.
Verification evidence showing resume prefers healthy materialized state and valid top-level next_action.
A code-to-plan traceability summary for exact resume behavior.
Implementation changes that align ops_* helper contracts with exact-active-transaction continuation and next_action-first resume behavior.

A traceability note or equivalent implementation summary defining the exact conditions for direct materialized-state resume, rebuild fallback, and explicit failure on malformed or ambiguous canonical persistence.
Aligned resume-related workflow guidance across helper, verify, commit, and response surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Resume never mints a new tx_id for an existing active transaction.
Resume never replaces the active transaction with a heuristic candidate when exact active state is available.
Healthy materialized state is the primary resume entrypoint.
Event-log rebuild is used only when materialized state is missing, incomplete, or inconsistent.
Malformed or ambiguous canonical persistence is treated as explicit failure rather than silent fallback or heuristic replacement when exact deterministic continuation is not safely possible.
Valid canonical next_action is preferred over status-derived heuristics and reconstructed guesses.
Post-terminal work is not resumed as active work.
Resume-related workflow guidance remains aligned across ops_* helpers, verify, commit, and response surfaces.
Exact-resume implementation does not promote ticket_id, task_id, session_id, or other helper context into canonical runtime transaction identity.

Ops_* helper behavior for exact-resume paths references docs/v0.6.0/ops-tool-contract-alignment-plan.md and does not introduce heuristic active-transaction replacement or status-over-next_action continuation.
